summary: RFC 2047 B-encode non-ASCII Subject and From-display headers in the Gmail-API RFC822 builder. Adds mime.BEncoding.Encode + (&mail.Address{}).String() wrapping at both sendViaGmailAPI and sendGmailWithRefresh; ASCII-only headers stay unchanged.
reason: 2026-05-22 Esper-to-Gradle send: subject contained 'Pricing -> consumption' (U+2192). Recipients saw the two-round CP-1252 -> UTF-8 mojibake cascade 'Pricing C-tilde-2 Aacute-...' on the wire. Root cause was raw concatenation of UTF-8 bytes onto RFC 5322 header lines, which mandates 7-bit ASCII; downstream MTAs each apply heuristic charset decoding, producing the cascade. mime.BEncoding.Encode short-circuits to the raw string for pure ASCII (no test regressions); wraps non-ASCII as =?UTF-8?B?...?= encoded-words that round-trip cleanly through net/mail.ParseAddress and mime.WordDecoder.
validated_outcome: send_rfc2047_test.go: TestEncodeRFC2047Subject_NoMojibakeSequence asserts the cascade fingerprint bytes are absent from the encoded output. TestGmailWirePayload_SubjectAndFromEncoded asserts the base64url-decoded RFC822 carries =?UTF-8?... for non-ASCII subject and 6 of 6 round-trip tests pass.
upstream_issue: TODO: file generator follow-up on cli-printing-press to fix the same defect in every printed CLI's Gmail raw-body path
